english: head, the ears, the neck, the upper arm, the wrists, and the feet; rus ͦbol-ba (rus 'bol ba) mentioned as a morbid symptom Lt.? - 2. the stone of apricots and other stone-fruits C., W.; grape-stone Wdn. - 3. energy, snyiṅ-gi (snying gi) Mil., gen. snyiṅ-rus (snying rus) q.v. - 4. v. rus (rus). Comp. rus-kraṅ (rus krang) skeleton, *rus-táṅ tsóg-se* (rus tang tshog se) W. he is nothing but skin and bones. - rús-k'u (rus khu) Lt. bone-broth(?). - rus-gróg (rus grog) Sch.: a dry bone (?). - rus-bcúd (rus bcud) Lt.? - rus-nád (rus nad) W. caries. - rús-bu (rus bu) 1. small bone. 2. bones in general Dzl. - rus-tsád (rus tshad), rus-tsód (rus tshod) Med.? - rus-šiṅ (rus shing) 1. Sch. firmness, perseverance, repentance. 2. n. of a part of the body (?) Lt.

1. indefinite num. or pron., single, a single one, some (persons), something; one to each, one at a time, re-ré (re re) or re (re) every, every one, every body, each, ráṅ-la bu re méd-na yid-ͦpam-pa re yóṅ-gi ͦdug, dés-na k'yéd-la-aṅ bu re dgos (rang la bu re med na yid 'pham pa re yong gi 'dug, des na khyed la ang bu re dgos) despair comes from having no son, therefore you, too, should have a son Mil.; yud re (yud re) for a moment, = yud tsam (yud tsam) Thgr.; lan re lan γnyis (lan re lan gnyis) once or twice Mil.; mi brgya re tsam żon čóg-pa (mi brgya re tsam zhon chog pa) (a horse) sufficiently (large) for being mounted by about a hundred men Glr.; lo re tsam ma-γtógs (lo re tsam ma gtogs) with the exception of one year about Glr.; ras-gós-rkyaṅ re (ras gos rkyang re) a single cotton garment Mil.; čos-ͦbrél re (chos 'brel re) a small amount of spiritual instruction Mil.; W.: *bal re* (bal re) some wool, *šú-gu re* (shu gu re) some paper (= żig (zhig)), *kú-šu re* (ku shu re) some apples; bčú-la p'úr-pa re γtád-nas (bcu la phur pa re gtad nas) handing to each of the ten a p'úr-pa (phur pa) Pth.; lág-na dóṅ-tse re-ré yod (lag na dong tse re re yod) in each of his hands there was a gold-coin Dzl.; nyín ré-la séms-čan k'ri re bsad (nyin re la sems can khri re bsad) Glr. he slaughtered every day 10 000 living beings, ra lṅa lṅa bsad (ra lnga lnga bsad) five goats (every day); mi res lug re bsad (mi res lug re bsad) each man killed one sheep Glr.; in a somewhat different sense: *lo ré-ne lo re čuṅ-se yod* (lo re ne lo re chung se yod) W. they grow smaller from year to year; nyuṅ re (nyung re) little at a time Glr.; re-re γnyis-γnyis (re re gnyis gnyis) one and all, one with another, indiscriminately Mil., re-re-bżín-gyi mgo (re re bzhin gyi mgo) every single person's head Tar.; re żig (re zhig) somebody, something; some (persons), a little; (with or without dus (dus)) a little while, re żig sdod (re zhig sdod) wait a little! Dzl.; re żig čig-na (re zhig cig na) after a little while, Bhar. 37; once, one day, one time, at a future time, also dus re żig-gi tse (dus re zhig gi tshe) Pth - 2. mutual, reciprocal (in this sense it is perh. to be spelled res (res), though it is certainly cognate to re (re)), dpon slob re ͦk'áṅ byuṅ (dpon slob re 'khang byung) Mil. there arises mutual discord between teachers and disciples Mil.; different, differing? ré-lta-bu (re lta bu) 'of a different kind or nature' Sch. - 3. sbst. a. the wooden parts of a door, re bżi (re bzhi) the four parts of a door-frame, yá-re (ya re) the head-piece, the lintel, má-re (ma re) the sill or threshold (= yá-tem (ya tem) and má-tem (ma tem)), *yá-re má-re ḍal toṅ* (ya re ma re dal tong) W. pull it down entirely! logs-ré (logs re) the side posts (C. sgo-ru (sgo ru)). b. v. re-mos (re mos) and reu (re'u). - 4. In such forms as mór-ra-re (mor ra re), mčís-sa-re (mchis sa re), gyúr-ta-re (gyur ta re) (Dzl. ༧༢, 1. ༧༩, 9. ༧༡, 2) it may be rendered by an adverb, as: certainly, undoubtedly. - 5. vb., v. réd-pa (red pa) and ré-ba (re ba). - 6. particle, mostly put between two closely connected words: nyams-re-dgá (nyams re dga'), blo-re-bdé (blo re bde) Glr., ͦo-re brgyál ('o re brgyal), skyug-re-lóg (skyug re log), ͦje-re-ͦjígs ('je re 'jigs), yi-re-múg (yi re mug), don-re-čúṅ (don re chung), snyiṅ-re-rjé (snying re rje) (this last very frq.), without essentially modifying the signification, yet only used in emphatic speech. - 7. num. for drug-ču (drug cu) in the abbreviated forms of the numbers 61 to 69. - 8. num. figure: 115.

(etymology?), acc. to the passages which came to my knowledge a strong negative (like οὐ μή), by no means, never, yoṅ re-skan (yong re skan) Mil. frq., that can never happen, that is absolutely impossible (parallel to yoṅ mi srid (yong mi srid)); tsím-par γyur re-skán (tshim par gyur re skan) they never can be satisfied with it Tar.
n. of a bitter medicinal herb.
Sch.: re-k'áṅ byúṅ-ba (re khang byung ba) to be not too much (?).
